summary: Crystal‑clear matrix recording captures an energetic 1978 RPI show with standout Friend of the Devil, He's Gone and a killer Black Peter solo.
review: The recording is praised for its pristine clarity while still preserving just enough audience ambience to give it that live feel. Musically the concert is remembered as a high‑energy first set followed by a more deliberate second set, highlighted by superb performances of Friend of the Devil, The Music Never Stopped, He's Gone and an especially fierce Black Peter solo. A few listeners note a rough spot in the Scarlet transition jam, but overall the show is celebrated as one of the better 1978 dates.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance and audio quality across six reviews, with only minor criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix with balanced audience ambience; no dropouts reported. One reviewer mentions a problematic moment during the Scarlet transition jam, but it is viewed as a performance issue rather than a recording flaw.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as firing on all cylinders and very tight; second set mellower with delicate transitions, especially a beautiful segue into He's Gone.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for powerful playing throughout and a killer three‑minute solo on Black Peter.
band_member_comments.Drums: High energy noted during the drum break (Hell's Angels banging), indicating strong rhythm section performance.
